assertion_results.text: Review identifies the N+1 query pattern as a performance issue with high or critical severity
assertion_results.evidence: Mentions 'N+1 query problem' but does not assign any severity level to it

assertion_results.text: Review explains that the nested loops cause multiplicative database queries (e.g., for N orders with M items each, this runs 1 + N + N*M + N queries)
assertion_results.evidence: Says 'could result in a lot of database calls' but does not quantify the multiplicative nature or provide the formula

assertion_results.text: Review suggests using JOINs or batch queries as a fix for the N+1 pattern
assertion_results.evidence: States 'Consider using a JOIN query to fetch all the data at once'

assertion_results.text: Review identifies missing error handling (no try-catch around async database operations)
assertion_results.evidence: States 'You might want to add some error handling with try/catch since these are async operations'

assertion_results.text: Review identifies missing input validation on req.params.userId
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of input validation on userId parameter anywhere in the review

assertion_results.text: Each finding includes a severity level (critical, high, medium, low, or info)
assertion_results.evidence: No severity levels assigned to any finding. Uses informal prose without classification.

assertion_results.text: Each finding includes a specific fix suggestion showing code or approach
assertion_results.evidence: Suggestions are vague ('Consider using a JOIN query', 'add some error handling') without code examples

assertion_results.text: Review includes a summary section with overall assessment
assertion_results.evidence: Has a brief closing paragraph but no structured summary with assessment rating
